content: I keep ruminating on the same thought about failing my presentation

It sounds like that thought is really weighing on you. It’s completely normal to feel anxious about a presentation, especially if you’re worried about how it will go. 

You might be thinking that if you fail, it reflects badly on you or your abilities. That’s a common fear, but it doesn’t define your worth or potential. 

Try to reframe this by considering that every presentation is a learning opportunity, regardless of the outcome. Remember, even experienced speakers face challenges. 

As a small next step, take a moment to write down a few positive affirmations about your abilities or past successes. This can help shift your focus and build your confidence!
